core_mechanism: a free-energy-driven spontaneous symmetry-breaking phase transition on a dynamical network, in which a high-temperature, maximally-symmetric, highly-connected disordered state gives way at low temperature to an ordered configuration selected by minimizing free energy (energy-vs-entropy competition)
brief_reason: The abstract describes states as dynamical graphs that are highly connected and permutation-symmetric at high energy but break that symmetry into an ordered, stable low-energy phase whose stability follows from minimizing the free energy of the dominant dynamical terms.
